name: GitHub
description: Git hosting and collaboration platform with extensive REST and GraphQL APIs for repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, apps, and more.

auth_methods.note: Docs explicitly list personal access tokens (classic) (used like API keys/tokens), OAuth access tokens (oauth2), GitHub Apps using JWTs and installation access tokens, and mention basic auth has been deprecated (recorded). 'api_key' is represented by personal access tokens; 'token' by installation/access tokens; 'other' covers fine-grained PATs and Actions tokens.

credential_access.note: Docs show steps for a signed-in user to generate a personal access token (no human approval). Account signup is free (https://github.com/join) and many developer features are available on Free plan (https://github.com/pricing).

api_surface.note: Developer docs root links to REST (https://docs.github.com/en/rest) and GraphQL; REST docs show wide coverage of product objects (repos, issues, pulls, actions, apps).

buildability.note: Public docs and self-serve credentials (PATs, OAuth app registration, GitHub App keys) exist and can be used immediately to build integrations. Evidence: REST auth docs, PAT creation, OAuth app creation, GitHub Apps auth pages.

queries: GitHub REST API authenticating to the REST API
Creating a personal access token GitHub docs
Authenticating with a GitHub App docs
Creating an OAuth App GitHub docs
